replies.author: @SeniorKlean
replies.text:
​@jackschwartz1783 Just calling something socialism is not an argument.  
Fiat does not exist because of the Fed or IMF.  It has existed for thousands of years whereas those institutions have existed for less than 100.  Independent central banking is a method of reducing the volatility of a fiat system.  In the grand scheme of human history, they haven't been around very long, but so far they seem to work rather well.  Yes, there still is volatility.  But compare the post Brenton Woods era to the 100 years before WWII.   
A gold standard very well may be a good thing.  I'm not even going to bother making a case for or against it because it's not going to happen without some very bad things happening first.

​@jackschwartz1783 Every time an empire becomes hegemonic they abandon the gold standard.  Rome abandoned it during the 3rd Century and continued to exist for another thousand years.  Spain abandoned it when colonial wealth distorted markets too much.   Britain abandoned it - silver in their case - after the Napoleonic wars.  China has used fiat nearly continuously for 1,500 years.  Then when the hegemon loses its power, the gold standard reaserts itself.

replies.text: Under international practice going back centuries, an attack on the embassy of a country was considered a _de facto_ declaration of war. In the traditional sense, Iran has been at war with the USA since 1979, when Iranian Revolutionary Guard proxies were allowed to attack the US embassy in Tehran, and hold the personnel hostage. No declaration of war required.
